dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6047 RH: Sharri Briley and Eric Edmundson Veterans Benefits Expansion Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Barrett (for himself, Mr. Bost, Mr. Van Orden, Mr. Ciscomani, Mrs. Radewagen, Ms. King-Hinds, Mr. Hudson, Mrs. Kiggans of Virginia, Mr. Hill of Arkansas, Mr. Luttrell, Mr. Wittman, and Mrs. Miller-Meeks) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs
Additional sponsors: Mr. Van Drew, Mr. Crank, and Mrs. Hinson
Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
action.action-instruction: Strike out all after the enacting clause and insert the part printed in italic
For text of introduced bill, see copy of bill as introduced on November 17, 2025

form.official-title: To amend title 38, United States Code, to direct the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to increase the dollar amounts for the payment of certain disability compensation and dependency and indemnity compensation under the laws administered by the Secretary.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Sharri Briley and Eric Edmundson Veterans Benefits Expansion Act of 2026.

section.header: Increase in rates of certain disability compensation and dependency and indemnity compensation under laws administered by Secretary of Veterans Affairs

subsection.header: Increase to rates of wartime disability compensation

paragraph.text: Section 1114 of title 38, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following new subsection:

subsection.text: In the case of a veteran eligible for a monthly aid and attendance allowance under subsection (r) or subsection (t) of this section, the Secretary shall, in addition to the total amount of compensation for which the veteran is eligible under this section, pay the veteran a supplemental monthly allowance at the rate of $833.33.

paragraph.text: Subsection (u) of such section (as added by paragraph (1)) shall take effect on December 1, 2026, and shall apply to months beginning on or after such date.

subsection.header: Increase to rates of dependency and indemnity compensation
subsection.text: Section 5312 of such title is amended by adding at the end the following new subsection:

paragraph.text: Whenever there is an increase in benefit amounts payable under title II of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 401 et seq.) as a result of a determination made under section 215(i) of such Act (42 U.S.C. 415(i)), the Secretary shall, except as provided in paragraph (2), effective on the date of such increase in benefit amounts, increase the dollar amounts in effect for the payment of dependency and indemnity compensation by the Secretary under paragraph (1) and paragraph (3) of section 1311(a) of this title, as such amounts were in effect immediately before the date of such increase in benefit amounts payable under title II of the Social Security Act, by a percentage equal to the sum of—

subparagraph.text: the percentage by which such benefit amounts are increased; and

paragraph.text: After the first increase under paragraph (1) to the dollar amounts in effect for the payment of dependency and indemnity compensation by the Secretary under paragraph (1) and paragraph (3) of section 1311(a) of this title, the Secretary shall carry out paragraph (1)(B) by substituting one half of one percent for one percent.

Whenever there is an increase under paragraph (1) in amounts in effect for the payment of dependency and indemnity compensation, the Secretary shall publish such amounts, as increased pursuant to such paragraph, in the Federal Register at the same time as the material required by section 215(i)(2)(D) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 415(i)(2)(D)) is published by reason of a determination under section 215(i) of such Act (42 U.S.C. 415(i)).

The requirement to increase, pursuant to paragraph (1), the amounts in effect for the payment of dependency and indemnity compensation under paragraph (1) and paragraph (3) of section 1311 (a) of this title by the Secretary shall—

subparagraph.text: take effect on December 1, 2026, and shall apply with respect to months beginning on or after such date; and
terminate after the date on which the second increase to such amounts pursuant to such paragraph occurs.

section.header: Modification of waivers of fees collected for housing loans guaranteed, insured, or made by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs
section.text: Section 3729(b)(2) of such title is amended, in the loan fee table—

paragraph.text: by striking June 9, 2034 each place it appears and inserting September 30, 2036;
in subparagraph (E), by striking 0.50 both places it appears and inserting 1.40; and
in subparagraph (I), by striking 0.50 each place it appears and inserting 1.0.

section.header: Extension of certain limits on payments of pension
section.text: Section 5503(d)(7) of such title is amended by striking January 31, 2033 and inserting September 30, 2036.

section.header: Home affordability for Guard and Reserve

subsection.text: This section may be cited as the Home Affordability for Guard and Reserve Act.

subsection.header: Eligibility of certain members of the reserve components and the National Guard for guaranteed housing loans

paragraph.header: Expanded definition of active duty for purposes of housing loans
paragraph.text: Section 3701(b) of title 38, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following new paragraph:
The term active duty has the meanings as follows:

subparagraph.text: In the case of members of the regular components of the Armed Forces, the meaning given such term in section 101(21)(A).
In the case of members of the reserve components of the Armed Forces—

clause.text: service on active duty (as defined in section 101(d) of title 10), inactive-duty training (as defined in section 101(d) of title 10), or annual training duty; or

service on active duty under a call or order to active duty under section 688, 12301(a), 12301(d), 12301(g), 12301(h), 12302, 12304, 12304a, or 12304b of title 10 or section 713 of title 14, but not including inactive duty training (as defined in section 101(d) of title 10) or annual training duty.

subparagraph.text: In the case of a member of the Army National Guard of the United States or Air National Guard of the United States, in addition to service described in subparagraph (B), full-time service—

clause.text: in the National Guard of a State for the purpose of organizing, administering, recruiting, instructing, or training the National Guard;
in the National Guard when performing full-time National Guard duty (as defined in section 101 of title 32); or
in the National Guard when performing active duty (as defined in section 101 of title 32).

paragraph.text: The amendments made by this subsection shall apply with respect to any service performed on or after September 11, 2001.

subsection.header: Expansion of eligibility for guaranteed housing loans to certain additional personnel upon payment of additional loan fee

paragraph.header: Expansion to individuals with at least 14 days of service
paragraph.text: Section 3701(b) of title 38, United States Code, is amended by inserting after paragraph (7) the following new paragraph:
The term veteran also includes, for purposes of home loans (subject to the additional loan fee in section 3729(b)(4)(J) of this title), an individual who—

subparagraph.text: is not otherwise eligible for the benefits of this chapter;
has completed a total service of at least 14 days on active duty under paragraph (B) or (C) of paragraph (9); and
following completion of such service, continued to serve until the completion of entry level and skill training (as defined in section 3301(3) of this title).

paragraph.text: Section 3702(a)(2) of title 38, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: Each individual described in section 3701(b)(8) of this title.

paragraph.header: Additional loan fee for such individuals
paragraph.text: Section 3729(b)(4) of title 38, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

subparagraph.text: In the case of a housing loan in which the veteran has eligibility under section 3701(b)(8) of this title and does not otherwise have eligibility, the loan fee table in paragraph (2) shall be applied to the veteran or other obligor (as applicable) by adding 1.00 to the percentage in the table.

paragraph.text: The Secretary of Veteran Affairs shall provide information about this benefit to the Secretary of Defense to ensure that each member of a reserve component or a member of the Army National Guard of the United States or Air National Guard of the United States who completes entry level and skill training (as defined in section 3301(3) of title 38, United States Code) after the date of the enactment of this Act is notified of their eligibility for housing loan benefits under chapter 37 of such title, including eligibility (subject to the additional loan fee) under section 3701(b)(8) of such title.

endorsement.action-desc: Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
